NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. In You, O LORD, I put my trust; Let me never be ashamed; Deliver me in Your righteousness.
NKJV.2: Bow down Your ear to me, Deliver me speedily; Be my rock of refuge, A fortress of defense to save me.
NKJV.3: For You are my rock and my fortress; Therefore, for Your name’s sake, Lead me and guide me.
NKJV.4: Pull me out of the net which they have secretly laid for me, For You are my strength.
NKJV.5: Into Your hand I commit my spirit; You have redeemed me, O LORD God of truth.
NKJV.6: I have hated those who regard useless idols; But I trust in the LORD.
NKJV.7: I will be glad and rejoice in Your mercy, For You have considered my trouble; You have known my soul in adversities,
NKJV.8: And have not shut me up into the hand of the enemy;  You have set my feet in a wide place.
NKJV.9: Have mercy on me, O LORD, for I am in trouble; My eye wastes away with grief, Yes, my soul and my body!
NKJV.10: For my life is spent with grief, And my years with sighing; My strength fails because of my iniquity, And my bones waste away.
NKJV.11: I am a reproach among all my enemies, But especially among my neighbors, And am repulsive to my acquaintances; Those who see me outside flee from me.
NKJV.12: I am forgotten like a dead man, out of mind; I am like a broken vessel.
NKJV.13: For I hear the slander of many; Fear is on every side; While they take counsel together against me, They scheme to take away my life.
NKJV.14: But as for me, I trust in You, O LORD; I say, “You are my God.”
NKJV.15: My times are in Your hand; Deliver me from the hand of my enemies, And from those who persecute me.
NKJV.16: Make Your face shine upon Your servant; Save me for Your mercies’ sake.
NKJV.17: Do not let me be ashamed, O LORD, for I have called upon You; Let the wicked be ashamed;  Let them be silent in the grave.
NKJV.18: Let the lying lips be put to silence, Which speak insolent things proudly and contemptuously against the righteous.
NKJV.19: Oh, how great is Your goodness, Which You have laid up for those who fear You, Which You have prepared for those who trust in You In the presence of the sons of men!
NKJV.20: You shall hide them in the secret place of Your presence From the plots of man;  You shall keep them secretly in a pavilion From the strife of tongues.
NKJV.21: Blessed be the LORD, For He has shown me His marvelous kindness in a strong city!
NKJV.22: For I said in my haste, “I am cut off from before Your eyes”; Nevertheless You heard the voice of my supplications When I cried out to You.
NKJV.23: Oh, love the LORD, all you His saints! For the LORD preserves the faithful, And fully repays the proud person.
NKJV.24: Be of good courage, And He shall strengthen your heart, All you who hope in the LORD.

ESV.1: In you, O LORD, do I take refuge; let me never be put to shame; in your righteousness deliver me!
ESV.2: Incline your ear to me; rescue me speedily! Be a rock of refuge for me, a strong fortress to save me!
ESV.3: For you are my rock and my fortress; and for your name's sake you lead me and guide me;
ESV.4: you take me out of the net they have hidden for me, for you are my refuge.
ESV.5: Into your hand I commit my spirit; you have redeemed me, O LORD, faithful God.
ESV.6: I hate  those who pay regard to worthless idols, but I trust in the LORD.
ESV.7: I will rejoice and be glad in your steadfast love, because you have seen my affliction; you have known the distress of my soul,
ESV.8: and you have not delivered me into the hand of the enemy; you have set my feet in a broad place.
ESV.9: Be gracious to me, O LORD, for I am in distress; my eye is wasted from grief; my soul and my body also.
ESV.10: For my life is spent with sorrow, and my years with sighing; my strength fails because of my iniquity, and my bones waste away.
ESV.11: Because of all my adversaries I have become a reproach, especially to my neighbors, and an object of dread to my acquaintances; those who see me in the street flee from me.
ESV.12: I have been forgotten like one who is dead; I have become like a broken vessel.
ESV.13: For I hear the whispering of many — terror on every side! — as they scheme together against me, as they plot to take my life.
ESV.14: But I trust in you, O LORD; I say, “You are my God.”
ESV.15: My times are in your hand; rescue me from the hand of my enemies and from my persecutors!
ESV.16: Make your face shine on your servant; save me in your steadfast love!
ESV.17: O LORD, let me not be put to shame, for I call upon you; let the wicked be put to shame; let them go silently to Sheol.
ESV.18: Let the lying lips be mute, which speak insolently against the righteous in pride and contempt.
ESV.19: Oh, how abundant is your goodness, which you have stored up for those who fear you and worked for those who take refuge in you, in the sight of the children of mankind!
ESV.20: In the cover of your presence you hide them from the plots of men; you store them in your shelter from the strife of tongues.
ESV.21: Blessed be the LORD, for he has wondrously shown his steadfast love to me when I was in a besieged city.
ESV.22: I had said in my alarm,  “I am cut off from your sight.” But you heard the voice of my pleas for mercy when I cried to you for help.
ESV.23: Love the LORD, all you his saints! The LORD preserves the faithful but abundantly repays the one who acts in pride.
ESV.24: Be strong, and let your heart take courage, all you who wait for the LORD!

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Psalm of David. In You, O LORD, I have taken refuge; Let me never be ashamed; In Your righteousness deliver me.
NASB.2: Incline Your ear to me, rescue me quickly; Be to me a rock of strength, A stronghold to save me.
NASB.3: For You are my rock and my fortress; For Your name's sake You will lead me and guide me.
NASB.4: You will pull me out of the net which they have secretly laid for me, For You are my strength.
NASB.5: Into Your hand I commit my spirit; You have ransomed me, O LORD, God of truth.
NASB.6: I hate those who regard vain idols, But I trust in the LORD.
NASB.7: I will rejoice and be glad in Your lovingkindness, Because You have seen my affliction; You have known the troubles of my soul,
NASB.8: And You have not given me over into the hand of the enemy; You have set my feet in a large place.
NASB.9: Be gracious to me, O LORD, for I am in distress; My eye is wasted away from grief, my soul and my body [also].
NASB.10: For my life is spent with sorrow And my years with sighing; My strength has failed because of my iniquity, And my body has wasted away.
NASB.11: Because of all my adversaries, I have become a reproach, Especially to my neighbors, And an object of dread to my acquaintances; Those who see me in the street flee from me.
NASB.12: I am forgotten as a dead man, out of mind; I am like a broken vessel.
NASB.13: For I have heard the slander of many, Terror is on every side; While they took counsel together against me, They schemed to take away my life.
NASB.14: But as for me, I trust in You, O LORD, I say, 'You are my God.'
NASB.15: My times are in Your hand; Deliver me from the hand of my enemies and from those who persecute me.
NASB.16: Make Your face to shine upon Your servant; Save me in Your lovingkindness.
NASB.17: Let me not be put to shame, O LORD, for I call upon You; Let the wicked be put to shame, let them be silent in Sheol.
NASB.18: Let the lying lips be mute, Which speak arrogantly against the righteous With pride and contempt.
NASB.19: How great is Your goodness, Which You have stored up for those who fear You, Which You have wrought for those who take refuge in You, Before the sons of men!
NASB.20: You hide them in the secret place of Your presence from the conspiracies of man; You keep them secretly in a shelter from the strife of tongues.
NASB.21: Blessed be the LORD, For He has made marvelous His lovingkindness to me in a besieged city.
NASB.22: As for me, I said in my alarm, 'I am cut off from before Your eyes'; Nevertheless You heard the voice of my supplications When I cried to You.
NASB.23: O love the LORD, all you His godly ones! The LORD preserves the faithful And fully recompenses the proud doer.
NASB.24: Be strong and let your heart take courage, All you who hope in the LORD.

NLT.1:
O LORD, I have come to you for protection;
don’t let me be disgraced.
Save me, for you do what is right.
NLT.2:
Turn your ear to listen to me;
rescue me quickly.
Be my rock of protection,
a fortress where I will be safe.
NLT.3:
You are my rock and my fortress.
For the honor of your name, lead me out of this danger.
NLT.4:
Pull me from the trap my enemies set for me,
for I find protection in you alone.
NLT.5:
I entrust my spirit into your hand.
Rescue me, LORD, for you are a faithful God.
NLT.6:
I hate those who worship worthless idols.
I trust in the LORD.
NLT.7:
I will be glad and rejoice in your unfailing love,
for you have seen my troubles,
and you care about the anguish of my soul.
NLT.8:
You have not handed me over to my enemies
but have set me in a safe place.
NLT.9:
Have mercy on me, LORD, for I am in distress.
Tears blur my eyes.
My body and soul are withering away.
NLT.10:
I am dying from grief;
my years are shortened by sadness.
Sin has drained my strength;
I am wasting away from within.
NLT.11:
I am scorned by all my enemies
and despised by my neighbors —
even my friends are afraid to come near me.
When they see me on the street,
they run the other way.
NLT.12:
I am ignored as if I were dead,
as if I were a broken pot.
NLT.13:
I have heard the many rumors about me,
and I am surrounded by terror.
My enemies conspire against me,
plotting to take my life.
NLT.14:
But I am trusting you, O LORD,
saying, “You are my God!”
NLT.15:
My future is in your hands.
Rescue me from those who hunt me down relentlessly.
NLT.16:
Let your favor shine on your servant.
In your unfailing love, rescue me.
NLT.17:
Don’t let me be disgraced, O LORD,
for I call out to you for help.
Let the wicked be disgraced;
let them lie silent in the grave.
NLT.18:
Silence their lying lips —
those proud and arrogant lips that accuse the godly.
NLT.19:
How great is the goodness
you have stored up for those who fear you.
You lavish it on those who come to you for protection,
blessing them before the watching world.
NLT.20:
You hide them in the shelter of your presence,
safe from those who conspire against them.
You shelter them in your presence,
far from accusing tongues.
NLT.21:
Praise the LORD,
for he has shown me the wonders of his unfailing love.
He kept me safe when my city was under attack.
NLT.22:
In panic I cried out,
“I am cut off from the LORD!”
But you heard my cry for mercy
and answered my call for help.
NLT.23:
Love the LORD, all you godly ones!
For the LORD protects those who are loyal to him,
but he harshly punishes the arrogant.
NLT.24:
So be strong and courageous,
all you who put your hope in the LORD!

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. In you, Yahweh, I take refuge. Let me never be disappointed. Deliver me in your righteousness.
WEB.2: Bow down your ear to me. Deliver me speedily. Be to me a strong rock, a house of defense to save me.
WEB.3: For you are my rock and my fortress, therefore for your name’s sake lead me and guide me.
WEB.4: Pluck me out of the net that they have laid secretly for me, for you are my stronghold.
WEB.5: Into your hand I commend my spirit. You redeem me, Yahweh, God of truth.
WEB.6: I hate those who regard lying vanities, but I trust in Yahweh.
WEB.7: I will be glad and rejoice in your loving kindness, for you have seen my affliction. You have known my soul in adversities.
WEB.8: You have not shut me up into the hand of the enemy. You have set my feet in a large place.
WEB.9: Have mercy on me, Yahweh, for I am in distress. My eye, my soul, and my body waste away with grief.
WEB.10: For my life is spent with sorrow, my years with sighing. My strength fails because of my iniquity. My bones are wasted away.
WEB.11: Because of all my adversaries I have become utterly contemptible to my neighbors, A fear to my acquaintances. Those who saw me on the street fled from me.
WEB.12: I am forgotten from their hearts like a dead man. I am like broken pottery.
WEB.13: For I have heard the slander of many, terror on every side, while they conspire together against me, they plot to take away my life.
WEB.14: But I trust in you, Yahweh. I said, “You are my God.”
WEB.15: My times are in your hand. Deliver me from the hand of my enemies, and from those who persecute me.
WEB.16: Make your face to shine on your servant. Save me in your loving kindness.
WEB.17: Let me not be disappointed, Yahweh, for I have called on you. Let the wicked be disappointed. Let them be silent in Sheol.
WEB.18: Let the lying lips be mute, which speak against the righteous insolently, with pride and contempt.
WEB.19: Oh how great is your goodness, which you have laid up for those who fear you, which you have worked for those who take refuge in you, before the sons of men!
WEB.20: In the shelter of your presence you will hide them from the plotting of man. You will keep them secretly in a dwelling away from the strife of tongues.
WEB.21: Praise be to Yahweh, for he has shown me his marvelous loving kindness in a strong city.
WEB.22: As for me, I said in my haste, “I am cut off from before your eyes.” Nevertheless you heard the voice of my petitions when I cried to you.
WEB.23: Oh love Yahweh, all you his saints! Yahweh preserves the faithful, and fully recompenses him who behaves arrogantly.
WEB.24: Be strong, and let your heart take courage, all you who hope in Yahweh.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A psalm of David. [1] LORD, I seek refuge in you;
let me never be disgraced. ⓒ
Save me by your righteousness.
CSB17.2:
Listen closely to me; rescue me quickly.
Be a rock of refuge for me,
a mountain fortress to save me. ⓓ
CSB17.3:
For you are my rock and my fortress; ⓔ
you lead and guide me
for your name’s sake. ⓕ
CSB17.4:
You will free me from the net
that is secretly set for me,
for you are my refuge. ⓖ
CSB17.5:
Into your hand I entrust my spirit; ⓗ
you have redeemed me, [1] LORD, God of truth. ⓘ
CSB17.6:
I [2] hate those who are devoted to worthless idols,
but I trust in the LORD. ⓙ
CSB17.7:
I will rejoice and be glad in your faithful love
because you have seen my affliction.
You know the troubles of my soul ⓚ
CSB17.8:
and have not handed me over to the enemy. ⓛ
You have set my feet in a spacious place. ⓜ
CSB17.9:
Be gracious to me, LORD,
because I am in distress; ⓝ
my eyes are worn out from frustration —
my whole being [3] as well. ⓐ
CSB17.10:
Indeed, my life is consumed with grief
and my years with groaning; ⓑ
my strength has failed
because of my iniquity, [4]
and my bones waste away. ⓒ
CSB17.11:
I am ridiculed by all my adversaries
and even by my neighbors.
I am dreaded by my acquaintances;
those who see me in the street run from me. ⓓ
CSB17.12:
I am forgotten: gone from memory
like a dead person — like broken pottery. ⓔ
CSB17.13:
I have heard the gossip of many;
terror is on every side. ⓕ
When they conspired against me,
they plotted to take my life. ⓖ
CSB17.14:
But I trust in you, LORD;
I say, “You are my God.” ⓗ
CSB17.15:
The course of my life is in your power; ⓘ
rescue me from the power of my enemies
and from my persecutors. ⓙ
CSB17.16:
Make your face shine on your servant; ⓚ
save me by your faithful love. ⓛ
CSB17.17:
LORD, do not let me be disgraced when I call on you. ⓜ
Let the wicked be disgraced;
let them be quiet [5] [6] in Sheol. ⓝ
CSB17.18:
Let lying lips ⓐ
that arrogantly speak against the righteous
in proud contempt be silenced. ⓑ
CSB17.19:
How great is your goodness
that you have stored up for those who fear you
and accomplished in the sight of everyone [7]
for those who take refuge in you. ⓒ
CSB17.20:
You hide them in the protection of your presence;
you conceal them in a shelter
from human schemes,
from quarrelsome tongues. ⓓ
CSB17.21:
Blessed be the LORD,
for he has wondrously shown his faithful love to me
in a city under siege. ⓔ
CSB17.22:
In my alarm I said,
“I am cut off from your sight.” ⓕ
But you heard the sound of my pleading
when I cried to you for help. ⓖ
CSB17.23:
Love the LORD, all his faithful ones. ⓗ
The LORD protects the loyal,
but fully repays the arrogant. ⓘ
CSB17.24:
Be strong, and let your heart be courageous,
all you who put your hope in the LORD. ⓙ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. In You, O LORD, I have placed my trust and taken refuge;
Let me never be ashamed;
In Your righteousness rescue me.
AMP.2:
Incline Your ear to me, deliver me quickly;
Be my rock of refuge,
And a strong fortress to save me.
AMP.3:
Yes, You are my rock and my fortress;
For Your name's sake You will lead me and guide me.
AMP.4:
You will draw me out of the net that they have secretly laid for me,
For You are my strength and my stronghold.
AMP.5:
Into Your hand I commit my spirit;
You have redeemed me, O LORD, the God of truth and faithfulness.
AMP.6:
I hate those who pay regard to vain (empty, worthless) idols;
But I trust in the LORD [and rely on Him with unwavering confidence].
AMP.7:
I will rejoice and be glad in Your steadfast love,
Because You have seen my affliction;
You have taken note of my life's distresses,
AMP.8:
And You have not given me into the hand of the enemy;
You have set my feet in a broad place.
AMP.9:
Be gracious and compassionate to me, O LORD, for I am in trouble;
My eye is clouded and weakened by grief, my soul and my body also.
AMP.10:
For my life is spent with sorrow
And my years with sighing;
My strength has failed because of my iniquity,
And even my body has wasted away.
AMP.11:
Because of all my enemies I have become a reproach and disgrace,
Especially to my neighbors,
And an object of dread to my acquaintances;
Those who see me on the street run from me.
AMP.12:
I am forgotten like a dead man, out of mind;
I am like a broken vessel.
AMP.13:
For I have heard the slander and whispering of many,
Terror is on every side;
While they schemed together against me,
They plotted to take away my life.
AMP.14:
But as for me, I trust [confidently] in You and Your greatness, O LORD;
I said, "You are my God."
AMP.15:
My times are in Your hands;
Rescue me from the hand of my enemies and from those who pursue and persecute me.
AMP.16:
Make Your face shine upon Your servant;
Save me in Your lovingkindness.
AMP.17:
Let me not be put to shame, O LORD, for I call on You;
Let the wicked (godless) be put to shame, let them be silent in Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead).
AMP.18:
Let the lying lips be mute,
Which speak insolently and arrogantly against the [consistently] righteous
With pride and contempt.
AMP.19:
How great is Your goodness,
Which You have stored up for those who [reverently] fear You,
Which You have prepared for those who take refuge in You,
Before the sons of man!
AMP.20:
In the secret place of Your presence You hide them from the plots and conspiracies of man;
You keep them secretly in a shelter (pavilion) from the strife of tongues.
AMP.21:
Blessed be the LORD,
For He has shown His marvelous favor and lovingkindness to me [when I was assailed] in a besieged city.
AMP.22:
As for me, I said in my alarm,
"I am cut off from Your eyes."
Nevertheless You heard the voice of my supplications (specific requests)
When I cried to You [for help].
AMP.23:
O love the LORD, all you His godly ones!
The LORD preserves the faithful [those with moral and spiritual integrity]
And fully repays the [self-righteousness of the] arrogant.
AMP.24:
Be strong and let your hearts take courage,
All you who wait for and confidently expect the LORD.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
I run to you, God;
I run for dear life. Don't let me down!
Take me seriously this time!
MSG.2:
Get down on my level and listen,
and please—no procrastination!
Your granite cave a hiding place,
your high cliff aerie a place of safety.
MSG.3:
You're my cave to hide in,
my cliff to climb.
Be my safe leader,
be my true mountain guide.
MSG.4:
Free me from hidden traps;
I want to hide in you.
MSG.5:
I've put my life in your hands.
You won't drop me,
you'll never let me down.
MSG.6:
I hate all this silly religion,
but you, God, I trust.
MSG.7:
I'm leaping and singing in the circle of your love;
you saw my pain,
you disarmed my tormentors,
MSG.8:
You didn't leave me in their clutches
but gave me room to breathe.
MSG.9:
Be kind to me, God—
I'm in deep, deep trouble again.
I've cried my eyes out;
I feel hollow inside.
MSG.10:
My life leaks away, groan by groan;
my years fade out in sighs.
My troubles have worn me out,
turned my bones to powder.
MSG.11:
To my enemies I'm a monster;
I'm ridiculed by the neighbors.
My friends are horrified;
they cross the street to avoid me.
MSG.12:
They want to blot me from memory,
forget me like a corpse in a grave,
discard me like a broken dish in the trash.
MSG.13:
The street-talk gossip has me
"criminally insane"!
Behind locked doors they plot
how to ruin me for good.
MSG.14:
Desperate, I throw myself on you:
you are my God!
MSG.15:
Hour by hour I place my days in your hand,
safe from the hands out to get me.
MSG.16:
Warm me, your servant, with a smile;
save me because you love me.
MSG.17:
Don't embarrass me by not showing up;
I've given you plenty of notice.
Embarrass the wicked, stand them up,
leave them stupidly shaking their heads
as they drift down to hell.
MSG.18:
Gag those loudmouthed liars
who heckle me, your follower,
with jeers and catcalls.
MSG.19:
What a stack of blessing you have piled up
for those who worship you,
Ready and waiting for all who run to you
to escape an unkind world.
MSG.20:
You hide them safely away
from the opposition.
As you slam the door on those oily, mocking faces,
you silence the poisonous gossip.
MSG.21:
Blessed God!
His love is the wonder of the world.
Trapped by a siege,
MSG.22:
I panicked.
"Out of sight, out of mind," I said.
But you heard me say it,
you heard and listened.
MSG.23:
Love God, all you saints;
God takes care of all who stay close to him,
But he pays back in full
those arrogant enough to go it alone.
MSG.24:
Be brave. Be strong. Don't give up.
Expect God to get here soon.

NIV.1:
Psalm 31
For the director of music. A psalm of David.
In you, O Lord, I have taken refuge;
let me never be put to shame;
deliver me in your righteousness.
NIV.2:
Turn your ear to me,
come quickly to my rescue;
be my rock of refuge,
a strong fortress to save me.
NIV.3:
Since you are my rock and my fortress,
for the sake of your name lead and guide me.
NIV.4:
Free me from the trap that is set for me,
for you are my refuge.
NIV.5:
Into your hands I commit my spirit;
redeem me, O Lord, the God of truth.
NIV.6:
I hate those who cling to worthless idols;
I trust in the Lord.
NIV.7:
I will be glad and rejoice in your love,
for you saw my affliction
and knew the anguish of my soul.
NIV.8:
You have not handed me over to the enemy
but have set my feet in a spacious place.
NIV.9:
Be merciful to me, O Lord, for I am in distress;
my eyes grow weak with sorrow,
my soul and my body with grief.
NIV.10:
My life is consumed by anguish
and my years by groaning;
my strength fails because of my affliction,
and my bones grow weak.
NIV.11:
Because of all my enemies,
I am the utter contempt of my neighbors;
I am a dread to my friends—
those who see me on the street flee from me.
NIV.12:
I am forgotten by them as though I were dead;
I have become like broken pottery.
NIV.13:
For I hear the slander of many;
there is terror on every side;
they conspire against me
and plot to take my life.
NIV.14:
But I trust in you, O Lord;
I say, “You are my God.”
NIV.15:
My times are in your hands;
deliver me from my enemies
and from those who pursue me.
NIV.16:
Let your face shine on your servant;
save me in your unfailing love.
NIV.17:
Let me not be put to shame, O Lord,
for I have cried out to you;
but let the wicked be put to shame
and lie silent in the grave.
NIV.18:
Let their lying lips be silenced,
for with pride and contempt
they speak arrogantly against the righteous.
NIV.19:
How great is your goodness,
which you have stored up for those who fear you,
which you bestow in the sight of men
on those who take refuge in you.
NIV.20:
In the shelter of your presence you hide them
from the intrigues of men;
in your dwelling you keep them safe
from accusing tongues.
NIV.21:
Praise be to the Lord,
for he showed his wonderful love to me
when I was in a besieged city.
NIV.22:
In my alarm I said,
“I am cut off from your sight!”
Yet you heard my cry for mercy
when I called to you for help.
NIV.23:
Love the Lord, all his saints!
The Lord preserves the faithful,
but the proud he pays back in full.
NIV.24:
Be strong and take heart,
all you who hope in the Lord.

NRSVCE.1:
In you, O LORD, I seek refuge;
do not let me ever be put to shame;
in your righteousness deliver me.
NRSVCE.2:
Incline your ear to me;
rescue me speedily.
Be a rock of refuge for me,
a strong fortress to save me.
NRSVCE.3:
You are indeed my rock and my fortress;
for your name’s sake lead me and guide me,
NRSVCE.4:
take me out of the net that is hidden for me,
for you are my refuge.
NRSVCE.5:
Into your hand I commit my spirit;
you have redeemed me, O LORD, faithful God.
NRSVCE.6:
You hate those who pay regard to worthless idols,
but I trust in the LORD.
NRSVCE.7:
I will exult and rejoice in your steadfast love,
because you have seen my affliction;
you have taken heed of my adversities,
NRSVCE.8:
and have not delivered me into the hand of the enemy;
you have set my feet in a broad place.
NRSVCE.9:
Be gracious to me, O LORD, for I am in distress;
my eye wastes away from grief,
my soul and body also.
NRSVCE.10:
For my life is spent with sorrow,
and my years with sighing;
my strength fails because of my misery,
and my bones waste away.
NRSVCE.11:
I am the scorn of all my adversaries,
a horror to my neighbors,
an object of dread to my acquaintances;
those who see me in the street flee from me.
NRSVCE.12:
I have passed out of mind like one who is dead;
I have become like a broken vessel.
NRSVCE.13:
For I hear the whispering of many —
terror all around! —
as they scheme together against me,
as they plot to take my life.
NRSVCE.14:
But I trust in you, O LORD;
I say, “You are my God.”
NRSVCE.15:
My times are in your hand;
deliver me from the hand of my enemies and persecutors.
NRSVCE.16:
Let your face shine upon your servant;
save me in your steadfast love.
NRSVCE.17:
Do not let me be put to shame, O LORD,
for I call on you;
let the wicked be put to shame;
let them go dumbfounded to Sheol.
NRSVCE.18:
Let the lying lips be stilled
that speak insolently against the righteous
with pride and contempt.
NRSVCE.19:
O how abundant is your goodness
that you have laid up for those who fear you,
and accomplished for those who take refuge in you,
in the sight of everyone!
NRSVCE.20:
In the shelter of your presence you hide them
from human plots;
you hold them safe under your shelter
from contentious tongues.
NRSVCE.21:
Blessed be the LORD,
for he has wondrously shown his steadfast love to me
when I was beset as a city under siege.
NRSVCE.22:
I had said in my alarm,
“I am driven far from your sight.”
But you heard my supplications
when I cried out to you for help.
NRSVCE.23:
Love the LORD, all you his saints.
The LORD preserves the faithful,
but abundantly repays the one who acts haughtily.
NRSVCE.24:
Be strong, and let your heart take courage,
all you who wait for the LORD.

NET.1: In you, O LORD, I have taken shelter! Never let me be humiliated! Vindicate me by rescuing me!
NET.2: Listen to me! Quickly deliver me! Be my protector and refuge, a stronghold where I can be safe!
NET.3: For you are my high ridge and my stronghold; for the sake of your own reputation you lead me and guide me.
NET.4: You will free me from the net they hid for me, for you are my place of refuge.
NET.5: Into your hand I entrust my life; you will rescue me, O LORD, the faithful God.
NET.6: I hate those who serve worthless idols, but I trust in the LORD.
NET.7: I will be happy and rejoice in your faithfulness, because you notice my pain and you are aware of how distressed I am.
NET.8: You do not deliver me over to the power of the enemy; you enable me to stand in a wide open place.
NET.9: Have mercy on me, for I am in distress! My eyes grow dim from suffering. I have lost my strength.
NET.10: For my life nears its end in pain; my years draw to a close as I groan. My strength fails me because of my sin, and my bones become brittle.
NET.11: Because of all my enemies, people disdain me; my neighbors are appalled by my suffering — those who know me are horrified by my condition; those who see me in the street run away from me.
NET.12: I am forgotten, like a dead man no one thinks about; I am regarded as worthless, like a broken jar.
NET.13: For I hear what so many are saying, the terrifying news that comes from every direction. When they plot together against me, they figure out how they can take my life.
NET.14: But I trust in you, O LORD! I declare, “You are my God!”
NET.15: You determine my destiny! Rescue me from the power of my enemies and those who chase me.
NET.16: Smile on your servant! Deliver me because of your faithfulness!
NET.17: O LORD, do not let me be humiliated, for I call out to you! May evil men be humiliated! May they go wailing to the grave!
NET.18: May lying lips be silenced — lips that speak defiantly against the innocent with arrogance and contempt!
NET.19: How great is your favor, which you store up for your loyal followers! In plain sight of everyone you bestow it on those who take shelter in you.
NET.20: You hide them with you, where they are safe from the attacks of men; you conceal them in a shelter, where they are safe from slanderous attacks.
NET.21: The LORD deserves praise for he demonstrated his amazing faithfulness to me when I was besieged by enemies.
NET.22: I jumped to conclusions and said, “I am cut off from your presence!” But you heard my plea for mercy when I cried out to you for help.
NET.23: Love the LORD, all you faithful followers of his! The LORD protects those who have integrity, but he pays back in full the one who acts arrogantly.
NET.24: Be strong and confident, all you who wait on the LORD!
